7-maths-ch1.label: Ch 1: Large Number Around Us
7-maths-ch1.title: Class 7 Maths: Ch 1 - Large Numbers Around Us

questions.q: In the expression 83 - 14 + 6, what are the correct terms (after converting subtraction to addition of the inverse)?
questions.explain: Subtracting 14 is the same as adding its inverse -14. So 83 - 14 + 6 = 83 + (-14) + 6, giving terms 83, -14, and 6.

questions.q: Terms in an expression are the parts separated by which sign, once all subtractions are rewritten as additions?
questions.explain: Terms are the parts of an expression separated by a plus sign, after converting every subtraction into addition of the inverse.

questions.q: What is the value of 30 + 5 x 4, evaluated using the correct order (evaluate each term first, then add)?
questions.explain: The term 5 x 4 is evaluated first to get 20, and then added to 30, giving 30 + 20 = 50.

questions.q: Which property is illustrated by the equality 6 + (-4) = (-4) + 6?
questions.explain: Swapping the terms of an expression without changing its value is called the commutative property of addition.

questions.q: When brackets preceded by a negative sign are removed from an expression, what happens to the signs of the terms inside the bracket?
questions.explain: For example, 100 - (15 + 56) = 100 - 15 - 56. Both 15 and 56 flip sign to -15 and -56 once the bracket is removed.
First evaluate inside the bracket: 250 - 100 = 150. Then 500 - 150 = 350.

questions.q: Which expression is equal in value to 28 + (35 - 10)?
questions.explain: Since the bracket here is NOT preceded by a negative sign, the terms inside keep their signs: 28 + (35 - 10) = 28 + 35 - 10 = 53.

questions.q: Using the distributive property, 5 x (8 - 3) is equal to:
questions.explain: The multiple of a difference equals the difference of the multiples: 5x(8-3) = 5x8 - 5x3 = 40 - 15 = 25.
Removing a bracket preceded by a negative sign flips the sign of both terms inside: 24 - (6+4) = 24 - 6 - 4.

questions.q: Irfan bought a biscuit packet for Rs 18 and a toor dal packet for Rs 47. He paid Rs 100 to the shopkeeper. Which expression correctly calculates his change, and what is its value?
questions.explain: Total cost = 18 + 47 = 65. Using brackets correctly: 100 - (18 + 47) = 100 - 65 = Rs 35 change.
